titleStmt.title: Letter from Campbell to the Chief Crown Solicitor, 26 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Campbell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Campbell, on behalf of the Assistant Financial Secretary for the War Office, Sir Charles Harris (1864-1943), to Henry Arthur Wynne concerning private Michael McCarthy. According to Campbell McCarthy has falsely claimed for dependence allowance and given false testimony upon re-enlistment.Henry Arthur Wynne was the chief crown solicitor for Ireland following the death of Malchy Kelly (1850-1916).

correspAction.placeName: War Office, St. Martin's Place, London, England

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Solicitor General
Please advise as to a
prosecution
Henry Arthur Wynne
29.5:16 War Office, St. Martin's Place, London, W.C. 26th May, 1916. Sir, With reference to War Office letter of the 20th of July,
1915, No.46/690 (Accounts 3.) I am directed to transmit the
enclosed documents which have been forwarded to this Department
by the Board of Customs and Excise in connection with a claim
for a Dependant's Allowance for the father of 5282, Private
Micheal McCarthy, 3rd Leinster Regiment, and to request that
you will be so good as to take the necessary action with a view
to the prosecution of the soldier and dependant in accordance
with instructions contained in the letter quoted. It has been ascertained that Private McCarthy is
serving at Cork. The soldier was a reservist in the 3rd Bn. Royal
Irish Regiment at the date of mobilization but discharged on
the 25th June, 1915 as no longer physically fit for war service.
Application for Dependants' Allowance was made by him and his
father in November 1914, and the documents in connection with
this claim are enclosed for reference. He reâenlisted in the Leinster Regiment on the 13th
January last, but did not disclose his previous military service
and the necessary action is being taken in connection with the
offence of false statements on attestation. I am, Sir,
Your obedient Servant, Wm. Campbell
for the Assistant Financial Secretary. The Chief Crown Solicitor, Dublin Castle.
